what_it_does: Records an image attachment entry in the design-assets channel, linking to a specific image asset by URI and noting when it was added.
observations: YAML front-matter metadata block with id att-d6699fc0
No body content beyond the front matter
standouts: Target uses an image:// URI pointing to design-assets/icons/image (34)
Timestamp includes microsecond precision and UTC offset
conventions: Uses YAML front matter for structured metadata
Includes id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields
References an external asset by URI rather than embedding it
notes: A ledger-style attachment record that links the design-assets channel to a specific image asset URI with an added timestamp.
questions: What does the numeric suffix '(34)' in the target URI signify?
How is the image:// URI resolved or served?
What process or message triggered this attachment being recorded?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it is a metadata record for an image attachment added to a channel.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to create a durable, timestamped record that a specific image asset is attached to the design-assets channel.
novelty: Uses an image:// URI target with a parenthetical numeric suffix for an icon asset.
contribution: Provides a typed, timestamped reference that links a channel to a specific image asset, which a fused system would keep as an attachment record.
summary_for_embedding: A channel attachment metadata file for the design-assets channel that records an image-type attachment targeting image://design-assets/icons/image (34) with an added UTC timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To log that a specific image asset was attached to the design-assets channel at a precise time.
